title: Fix infinite 'Loading your profile…' spinner on app load

requirements.summary: Fix the infinite loading spinner by ensuring ChatView exits the loading state within 10 seconds regardless of backend response
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Loading your profile...' spinner is never displayed for more than 10 seconds under any network or backend condition.
If the backend getUserProfile call resolves with a null/empty result, ChatView exits the loading state and shows the profile setup flow (DisplayNamePrompt or ProfileSetupModal).
If the backend getUserProfile call resolves with a valid profile, ChatView exits the loading state and renders the full main UI (message feed, friends sidebar, navigation).
If the backend getUserProfile call errors or times out, ChatView exits the loading state gracefully and shows an appropriate fallback (setup flow or an error message), not a permanent spinner.
On subsequent visits where a profile already exists, the app loads past the spinner quickly and renders the main chat UI without requiring any user action.
No unhandled promise rejections or console errors occur during the profile loading lifecycle.

file_operations.description: Fix the loading gate logic that displays the spinner. Change the condition from checking only `profileLoading` to also evaluating terminal query states. Ensure the loading gate evaluates to false when the profile query reaches any terminal state (isSuccess or isError), not just when userProfile is truthy. Add a 10-second timeout mechanism using setTimeout that forcibly exits the loading state if the query hasn't resolved. Update the loading condition to: show spinner only when (actorFetching || profileLoading) AND a timeout hasn't been reached AND the query hasn't reached a terminal state. Handle the case where userProfile is null (new user) or undefined (error/no data) by showing the profile setup flow instead of staying in the loading state.

requirements.summary: Audit and fix the getUserProfile hook to ensure it transitions out of loading state correctly when the actor is ready
acceptanceCriteria: The getUserProfile React Query hook has an 'enabled' condition that prevents it from firing before the actor is ready, and correctly transitions to a non-loading state once the actor resolves.
ChatView's loading gate (the condition that shows the spinner) evaluates to false as soon as the profile query reaches any terminal state (success or error), not just when data is truthy.
If the actor initialisation itself hangs, a timeout or fallback mechanism ensures the UI is never permanently blocked.

file_operations.description: Fix the useGetCallerUserProfile hook to properly handle actor initialization and loading states. Add an 'enabled' condition that checks both actor availability and actorFetching state: enabled: !!actor && !actorFetching. Return a custom loading state that properly reflects actor dependency: isLoading: actorFetching || query.isLoading, and isFetched: !!actor && query.isFetched. Ensure the query includes retry: false to avoid infinite retry loops. Add a staleTime configuration (e.g., 30000ms) to prevent unnecessary refetches. Verify the queryFn properly throws an error if actor is unavailable to trigger the error state rather than hanging indefinitely.
